struct PacketHeader {

        #include <hexi/hexi.h>
        #include <vector>
        #include <cstdint>

        bool write_data_with_check(std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer, uint32_t header, const std::vector<uint32_t>& items) {
            // Create a buffer_adaptor for the buffer
            hexi::buffer_adaptor adaptor(buffer);

            // Check available space
            auto available = adaptor.size();
            // Calculate required size: sizeof(header) + items.size() * sizeof(uint32_t)
            const auto required_size = sizeof(header) + items.size() * sizeof(uint32_t);

            if (available < required_size) {
                return false;
            }

            // Create a binary_stream that uses the buffer_adaptor
            hexi::binary_stream stream(adaptor);

            // Write the header
            stream << header;

            // Write the items
            stream << items;

            // Return true on success

```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>
#include <stdexcept>

struct PacketHeader {
    uint32_t magic;
    uint16_t version;
    uint16_t flags;
};

PacketHeader read_header_with_span(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    // The function parameter is const, but the Hexi library requires non-const for buffer_adaptor and binary_stream.
    // We cast away the constness. This is undefined behavior if the buffer is actually const.
    std::span<uint8_t> non_const_buffer = const_cast<std::span<uint8_t>>(buffer);

    // Create a buffer_adaptor from the non-const buffer.
    hexi::buffer_adaptor<std::span<uint8_t>> adaptor(non_const_buffer);

    // Check available data
    if (adaptor.size() < sizeof(PacketHeader)) {
        throw std::runtime_error("Buffer too small");
    }

    // Create a binary_stream from the adaptor.
    hexi::binary_stream<decltype(adaptor)> stream(adaptor);

    // Read the header fields
    PacketHeader header;
    stream >> header.magic >> header.version >> header.flags;

    return header;
}
```
